## [Note]

- dbbot v0.5.0 is the next official release focused on safer control-host upgrades, earlier VIP validation, and more reliable MySQL and Monitoring observability onboarding.

## [Add]

- Add staged progress output and a `--debug` mode to `dbbotctl release upgrade` and `dbbotctl release rollback` so operators can stream post-checks live when needed.
- Add host interface validation before VIP-related tasks run in MGR, MHA, master-slave keepalived, and InnoDB Cluster Router playbooks.
- Add automatic MGR topology detection for `mysqld_exporter` service rendering so Group Replication collectors can be enabled without manual topology overrides.

## [Change]

- Update the local curated package builder to include untracked non-ignored files under managed release paths and skip missing paths safely during packaging.
- Update `setup_portable_ansible.sh` to persist both `ansible` and `ansible-playbook` aliases and add the dbbot `bin/` directory to `PATH`.
- Update MySQL exporter target examples and registrar documentation to reflect `service_name`, `replication_set`, and `topology` label usage.

## [Fix]

- Replace the MySQL control-host `tar` prerequisite flow with raw local commands so package validation does not depend on Python yum or dnf bindings.
- Rework Monitoring package pre-checks to reuse the shared local archive preparation flow and honor `fcs_auto_download` for Prometheus, Grafana, and Alertmanager archives.
- Refresh `exporterregistrar` so MySQL targets write `service_name`, and `--force` now updates existing target labels instead of leaving stale metadata behind.
- Reconfigure `mysqld_exporter` even when already installed so topology-driven collector changes are applied to existing instances and MGR dashboards can receive Group Replication metrics.
